name: dpkt (Fast Packet Creation and Parsing)

performance.notes: Pure Python with minimal overhead; fast for packet parsing; no subprocess

agent_readiness.notes: Low-level packet creation and parsing without subprocess. import dpkt. with open('file.pcap', 'rb') as f: pcap = dpkt.pcap.Reader(f); for ts, buf in pcap: eth = dpkt.ethernet.Ethernet(buf); if isinstance(eth.data, dpkt.ip.IP): ip = eth.data; if isinstance(ip.data, dpkt.tcp.TCP): tcp = ip.data; src = socket.inet_ntoa(ip.src); dport = tcp.dport. Protocols: Ethernet, IP, IP6, TCP, UDP, ICMP, ARP, DNS, HTTP, SSL, SMTP. Packet crafting: pkt = dpkt.ethernet.Ethernet(dst=..., src=..., data=dpkt.ip.IP(...)). struct-like binary access. No TShark dependency.

gotchas: Low-level — must manually check isinstance() for each layer
No display filter syntax — must write own packet filtering logic
Documentation is minimal — read source or examples
Protocol coverage less than Wireshark/Scapy — custom protocols need manual parsing
best_when: High-performance PCAP parsing without subprocess overhead
Security research requiring fast iteration over large PCAP files
Packet crafting and building custom protocol sequences
When Scapy's overhead is too high for batch analysis
avoid_when: Complex protocol dissection (use PyShark with Wireshark dissectors)
Live capture (use Scapy or libpcap directly)
When Wireshark filter syntax is needed
summary: dpkt parses and creates packets at low level without subprocess. pcap.Reader(f): for ts, buf in pcap: Ethernet(buf).data → IP → TCP. isinstance() chain per layer. Protocols: Ethernet, IP, TCP, UDP, ICMP, DNS, HTTP, SSL. Packet crafting: Ethernet(data=IP(data=TCP())). BSD-3 by Dug Song. Best for fast PCAP analysis. No TShark. Low-level isinstance() checks. Minimal docs. Less protocol coverage than Scapy.
